_doc: The capability-module registry — the one home for the kit `setup` offers and the assemblers compose. A capability module is a TOGGLEABLE bundle whose parts live in their existing single homes (a config toggle · per-role prompt FRAGMENTS · optionally a deny rule or quality check); this file catalogs them. A module's FLOOR (the part that is never off) is NOT here — it stays in the role's floor body (src/agents/<role>.md); this registry carries only the DEEPENING (toggle · per-kind defaults · targets · fragment pointers). The assembler (src/adapter/modules.mjs, shared by both install-agents shims) replaces each role's `<!-- ai-pm:modules -->` marker with the ENABLED modules' fragments, in REGISTRY ORDER. HONESTY: a module is `[persona]` prompt content — it sharpens what a role THINKS about; it blocks nothing mechanically unless it also carries a deny rule. Fail-safe: an unknown/malformed toggle, or an unknown/absent project kind, resolves the module ON with the strict-side defaults (a bad config can never silently disable the rigor). Adding a module = a row here + its src/modules/<id>/ fragments; the assemblers compose it for free — NO core edit (docs/architecture.md `## Capability modules`).

_row_shape.id: short stable name — matches the src/modules/<id>/ dir and the ai-pm.config.json modules.<id> toggle key
_row_shape.for: one plain line: what this module gives the project
_row_shape.toggle: the SHAPE of the config value under modules.<id> — e.g. { depth: 'rich | light', nonSecurity: bool }. A literal `false` is the only OFF; anything else (true, object, malformed, absent) ⇒ ON (fail-safe to more rigor)
_row_shape.defaults: per project kind, the default toggle this module runs with; unknown/absent kind ⇒ the strict side (docs/architecture.md)
_row_shape.targets: [{ role, beat }] — which role agent the module deepens, at which loop beat (plan | review)
_row_shape.fragments: { <role>: 'src/modules/<id>/<role>.md' } — registry-relative pointers (absolute or `..`-bearing is REJECTED at assembly); the per-role prompt the module composes in

modules.for: Explicit threat enumeration for a security-relevant change — attack surface, data/secret exposure, trust boundaries — deepening the always-on Reviewer security item and adding a plan-time threat pass to the Builder.

Plan-time product discovery for a user-facing change — who it is for, the user pain, whether it is the right bet, the cheapest test, what breaks if it is NOT built — deepening the always-on Builder plan product-question item and the Reviewer's check that the shipped change serves its plan's user claim.
